title: Measure host run-to-run noise floor on ML1M
description: New row (was inside cancelled SPRINT-11 scope): run 2-3 core PreferGrow seeds on ML1M under the identical clean-root protocol to measure the run-to-run noise floor of delta_test_p2_ndcg10; this is the required input for attributing the Gate-1 red flag (-0.0151) and for freezing Gate-P0 thresholds.
acceptance_criteria: WHEN 2-3 core seeds complete on ML1M THEN a dated artifact under docs/reports/data reports per-seed test p2 ndcg10 and the max pairwise absolute delta as the noise floor; WHEN the floor is known THEN the Gate-1 red-flag note in paper/main_v2.tex is resolved to either implementation-issue or within-noise wording via a documented decision line; WHEN the floor informs BRIDGE thresholds THEN BRIDGE-02 cites the artifact; ref: docs/superpowers/specs/2026-07-06-evidence-priced-schedule-design.md:4
review_initial_requirements: Check seeds differ only in random_seed and reuse the frozen clean-root protocol and selector.
review_regression_requirements: Verify the red-flag resolution wording claims nothing beyond the measured floor.

title: Conditionally rerun the Beauty corruption chain under the frozen v2 gate
description: Supersedes old SPRINT-08 with an explicit condition: run only if l20 recovers by 2026-07-10; otherwise the paper keeps the first-generation corruption evidence plus the FOLLOWUP-10 observational readout with explicit scoping.
acceptance_criteria: WHEN l20 recovers on or before 2026-07-10 THEN v2 full and text_anchor_only and u_shuffle rerun on the frozen corrupted Beauty banks and clean-vs-corrupted deltas are tabulated; WHEN l20 recovers later THEN this row closes as documented-gap and the paper robustness claim stays scoped to the first-generation instantiation; WHEN either outcome lands THEN paper/main_v2.tex pending marker for SPRINT-08 is replaced accordingly; ref: docs/superpowers/specs/2026-07-06-evidence-priced-schedule-design.md:0.2
review_initial_requirements: Check corrupted banks are the existing frozen artifacts with unchanged corruption seeds.
review_regression_requirements: Verify the paper wording matches whichever branch actually executed.

title: Run one external baseline (DiffuRec or RecFormer) across four datasets
description: Supersedes old FOLLOWUP-13 narrowed to a single baseline, single round: choose by fastest credible integration with the regenerated splits and shared selector; produce baseline-vs-host-vs-ours NDCG@10/HR@10 table.
acceptance_criteria: WHEN the baseline is chosen THEN the choice rationale (integration cost, comparability) is recorded in this row; WHEN runs complete on Steam, ML1M, Beauty, ATG THEN a dated table under shared regenerated splits and shared selector is archived under docs/reports/data; WHEN l20 stays down past 2026-07-13 THEN this row closes as documented-gap and the paper setup wording records the absence honestly; ref: docs/superpowers/specs/2026-07-06-evidence-priced-schedule-design.md:0.2
review_initial_requirements: Check the baseline consumes the regenerated splits and does not use its own preprocessing.
review_regression_requirements: Verify no baseline number is copied from another paper.

title: Freeze the Gate 2 claim-strength decision on 2026-07-14
description: Supersedes old SPRINT-12 unchanged in substance: assemble whatever P0/P1 artifacts exist on 7/14, select exactly one exit (strong/medium/weak), and freeze final claim wording. Gate-1 readout already shows only the weak exit reachable unless CLOSE-02 reattributes the ML1M miss to noise.
acceptance_criteria: WHEN 2026-07-14 arrives THEN a dated Gate 2 report selects exactly one exit with the evidence table inline and quotes the Gate-1 reachability analysis; WHEN the exit is chosen THEN paper claim sentences are frozen per the Family D upgrade-only rule and stronger claims are forbidden in later edits; WHEN CLOSE-02 shows the ML1M delta within the host noise floor THEN the report may document a medium-exit upgrade with the noise-floor artifact cited; ref: docs/superpowers/specs/2026-07-06-evidence-priced-schedule-design.md:5.2
review_initial_requirements: Check the decision uses only dated artifacts under docs/reports/data.
review_regression_requirements: Verify the frozen wording is consistent with the freeze memo and the chosen exit.

title: Finalize the repositioned v2 draft and submit the AAAI-27 abstract
description: The new draft paper/main_v2.tex (EN) + paper/main_v2_zh.md (CN mirror) was produced on 2026-07-06 per spec 5.1; this row covers user review, title+abstract freeze by 7/18, OpenReview registration, and abstract submission by 7/20 (official DDL 7/21 UTC-12).
acceptance_criteria: WHEN the user approves the v2 draft THEN title and abstract are frozen by 2026-07-18 and match the Family D claim sentences verbatim where required; WHEN the abstract is submitted on OpenReview by 2026-07-20 THEN the submission id is recorded in this row; WHEN any wording stronger than the freeze memo appears THEN it must cite a dated upgrade memo or be reverted; ref: docs/superpowers/specs/2026-07-06-evidence-priced-schedule-design.md:5.1
review_initial_requirements: Check the abstract contains the frozen EN main sentence and the boundary sentence and no forbidden-list phrasing.
review_regression_requirements: Verify the submitted abstract equals the frozen one and the OpenReview record matches.

title: Backfill remaining numbers, compile final PDF, submit full paper and supplementary
description: Supersedes the rest of SPRINT-13: replace every remaining pending macro from dated artifacts only, regenerate figures, compile the camera-style anonymous PDF within 7 pages, submit full paper by 7/27 (DDL 7/28) and code/data supplement plus reproducibility checklist by 7/30 (DDL 7/31).
acceptance_criteria: WHEN figures and tables regenerate THEN every number cites a docs/reports/data artifact in a tex comment and no pending macro remains; WHEN the PDF compiles THEN technical content fits 7 pages with references and checklist outside the limit and the submission is anonymous; WHEN supplementary is packaged THEN code and data needed for reproducibility are included at submission time per AAAI-27 policy; ref: docs/superpowers/specs/2026-07-06-evidence-priced-schedule-design.md:5.2
review_initial_requirements: Check no placeholder is filled from memory instead of an artifact.
review_regression_requirements: Verify the compiled PDF respects the forbidden-claim list and the Gate-2 frozen exit wording.

title: Run the pre-submission vision review against the freeze discipline
description: Supersedes old REVIEW-01 with updated checks for the v2 draft and the new spec: same-model sub-agent review comparing delivered artifacts against the freeze memo, the Gate-2 exit, and the new-draft repositioning boundaries, finishing before the full-paper submission.
acceptance_criteria: WHEN all CLOSE rows except CLOSE-07 are closed or documented-gap THEN the review runs with these checks: (1) g==0 equivalence evidence is numeric against the real core path; (2) Gate 0/0-v2/1/2 verdicts cite dated artifacts and the claim strength matches the Gate-2 exit; (3) Prop-6 empirical wording stays paused unless CLOSE-02 cleared the red flag with a documented decision; (4) the paper contains no pending macros and respects the forbidden-claim list; (5) the submitted abstract and full paper do not drift qualitatively (official rejection clause); WHEN gaps are found THEN follow-up rows are appended before submission; ref: docs/superpowers/specs/2026-07-06-evidence-priced-schedule-design.md:5.2
review_initial_requirements: Verify all prior CLOSE rows are closed or documented-gap before running.
review_regression_requirements: Run the five listed checks and record pass or fail per check with artifact citations.

title: Produce the bilingual one-page research plan for Shanghai AI Lab outreach
description: Condense spec sections 1.3, 2, and 3 into a one-page research plan (CN and EN): AAAI-27 work in one sentence with the 4/4 inversion, the negative-results-to-schedule reasoning chain, the v3 method and theory program, and one-sentence hooks to Lumina-DiMOO and InternVideo open problems; attach to the July application email.
acceptance_criteria: WHEN the one-pager is drafted THEN it fits one page per language and contains no claim beyond archived artifacts; WHEN the user approves THEN it is exported alongside the CV for the admissions@pjlab.org.cn submission; WHEN team-specific hooks are written THEN Lumina and InternVideo variants differ only in the final paragraph; ref: docs/superpowers/specs/2026-07-06-evidence-priced-schedule-design.md:8
review_initial_requirements: Check every factual sentence traces to a dated artifact or the frozen spec.
review_regression_requirements: Verify the CN and EN versions state identical claims.

title: Write the v3 theory memo: L1-prime through P3-prime proofs, P2-prime derivation, frozen schedule family
description: Post-submission theory sprint: full proofs of the time-inhomogeneous rank-one composition lemma (L1'), the schedule-level reduction (P1'), and the TV bound with the effective cumulative gate (P3'); the P2-prime derivation of score-entropy validity and reversal form under time-varying proposals; freeze the schedule family parameters (a,b) and the Gate-P0 criteria before any run.
acceptance_criteria: WHEN the memo is finalized THEN L1'/P1'/P3' carry complete proofs and P2' carries either a complete proof or an explicit restriction to piecewise-constant schedules with the failure point documented; WHEN the schedule family is frozen THEN the (a,b) parameters and Gate-P0 pass criteria are recorded before BRIDGE-04 launches and never adjusted after; WHEN CLOSE-02 delivered a noise floor THEN the criteria cite it; ref: docs/superpowers/specs/2026-07-06-evidence-priced-schedule-design.md:3
review_initial_requirements: Check the proofs use the actual kernel algebra from the codebase conventions (row-vector, rank-one absorption) and not a re-derived variant.
review_regression_requirements: Verify no criterion or parameter is adjusted after seeing any BRIDGE-04 number.

title: Implement the time-dependent gate hook with equivalence test suites
description: Implement g(t) scheduling in the proposal path per spec 2.4: schedule-aware proposal construction, effective-proposal (p-bar) closed form in the four kernel call sites, config plumbing, and two equivalence suites (s==1 equals v2-without-u-tilde; phi==0 equals host for any s).
acceptance_criteria: WHEN the hook is implemented THEN non-schedule configs are byte-identical in behavior to current v2 and all existing tests still pass; WHEN the two equivalence suites run THEN both pass numerically against the real code paths; WHEN the manifest is extended THEN it records the schedule family, parameters, and the frozen theory-memo hash; ref: docs/superpowers/specs/2026-07-06-evidence-priced-schedule-design.md:2.4
review_initial_requirements: Check the effective proposal uses the L1-prime closed form rather than per-step matrix products.
review_regression_requirements: Verify pseudo-item mass handling is untouched and u_shuffle ablation semantics are preserved for the ablation arm.

title: Run the decisive schedule experiment and issue the Gate-P0 verdict
description: Run the pre-registered decisive experiment: {const, early-fade, late-fade} x {Steam, Beauty} plus the frozen host reference, one seed, shared selector; evaluate against the BRIDGE-02 frozen criteria and record continue-or-kill for the v3a direction.
acceptance_criteria: WHEN the six runs complete THEN a dated report tabulates delta_test_p2_ndcg10 per arm against host and against the const arm; WHEN the frozen criteria are applied THEN the verdict quotes them verbatim with margins and decides continue or kill with no post-hoc reframing; WHEN Beauty parity fails THEN an implementation red flag is recorded and the verdict is deferred until resolved; ref: docs/superpowers/specs/2026-07-06-evidence-priced-schedule-design.md:4
review_initial_requirements: Check all arms share splits, banks, selector, and seed discipline with the SPRINT-05 official protocol.
review_regression_requirements: Verify the verdict maps each arm to its pre-registered criterion and the kill path is honored if criteria fail.
